flashcards.back: A binary heap is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property. In a max-heap, parent node is greater than or equal to children; in min-heap, parent is less than or equal to children.

The heap property states that for every node (except root), the key value of the parent node is either greater than or equal to (max-heap) or less than or equal to (min-heap) the key values of its children.

flashcards.front: What is a complete binary tree?
flashcards.back: A complete binary tree is a tree where all levels except possibly the last are completely filled, and all nodes are as far left as possible.
MAX-HEAP: Parent key ≥ children keys. MIN-HEAP: Parent key ≤ children keys. Used for descending and ascending order sorting respectively.
Heapify is the process of maintaining the heap property by comparing a node with its children and swapping with the larger child if the heap property is violated. It is a bottom-up procedure.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of heapify?
flashcards.back: Heapify runs in O(log n) time because it traverses from root to at most one path of height of the heap (log n levels).

flashcards.front: How do you build a max-heap from an unsorted array?
flashcards.back: Call heapify on all non-leaf nodes (from floor(n/2)-1 down to 0) in reverse order. This builds the heap in O(n) time.

flashcards.front: What are the steps of Heapsort algorithm?
flashcards.back: 1) Build max-heap from input array. 2) Swap root (max element) with last element. 3) Reduce heap size by 1. 4) Heapify root. 5) Repeat steps 2-4 until heap size is 1.
Best, Average, and Worst case: O(n log n). Building heap takes O(n), and each of n extractions takes O(log n).
O(1) auxiliary space. Heapsort is an in-place sorting algorithm, though some implementations use O(log n) for recursion stack.

flashcards.front: Why is Heapsort better than Quicksort for worst-case?
flashcards.back: Quicksort can degrade to O(n²) with poor pivot selection, while Heapsort guarantees O(n log n) regardless of input distribution.

flashcards.front: What is the height of a heap with n nodes?
flashcards.back: Height = floor(log₂n). Since heap is a complete binary tree, height is always O(log n).

flashcards.front: What is the index formula for children in array representation?
flashcards.back: For index i: Left child = 2i + 1, Right child = 2i + 2. Parent of node at index i = floor((i-1)/2).
An abstract data type where elements have priorities. Highest priority element is served first. Binary heaps efficiently implement priority queues with O(log n) insert and extract-max operations.

flashcards.front: What are the applications of heaps?
flashcards.back: Priority queues, job scheduling systems, order statistics (finding kth largest/smallest), Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm, Huffman coding (for priority queue in merging files).
